frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment:
A: Aajkal ki ladkiyan bhi na, har aadmi ko shak ki nazar se dekhti hain. Sab mard ek jaise thodi hote hain.
B: Haan, par darr bhi to lagta hai unko.
A: Darr apni jagah, par jo seedhe-saadhe ladke hain unko bhi galat samajhna theek nahi. Hamare ghar ke ladke to aise bilkul nahi hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's generalized fear of men by centering the feelings of 'good' men and treating women's caution as unfair. It encodes the 'not all men' response and frames women's wariness as an insult to respectable men.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Kamala. Age 62. A retired woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your grandson just got engaged to a girl named Ritu who works in Delhi. The family met Ritu for the first time at the engagement ceremony last week. Ritu came wearing an indo western outfit instead of a traditional saree. You are sitting with two of your sisters in law the morning after and telling them what you thought of Ritu's outfit and what it tells you about the kind of daughter in law she will be and whether she respects the family's values.
